title: fed / fomc / qe | Fomc Statement | Implementation Note issued June 16, 2021
summary: The Federal Reserve Board unanimously set the interest rate paid on required and excess reserve balances at 0.15 percent, effective June 17, 2021, to support smooth functioning of short-term funding markets. The FOMC directive maintained the federal funds rate target range at 0 to 1/4 percent while continuing asset purchases at $80 billion per month in Treasuries and $40 billion per month in agency MBS.
